signals.reasoning: This live long has slipped back under the profit-retention threshold, keeping only about 73% of peak P&L earlier and now flagged by a VETO regime. In a position-management round, I would protect the remaining gain rather than keep hoping through macro/geopolitical noise.
BTC is liquid and stable, but the board remains no-trade and the active veto argues against opening fresh exposure. Neutral is still the right posture.
ETH is mildly constructive but still low-edge, with only a neutral board read and no compelling asymmetry.
SOL is a bit stronger than ETH on price, but the board still marks it no-trade and positive funding keeps it from becoming a clean squeeze name.
TNSR still has a real squeeze-continuation profile mechanically, but the active veto is a direct reason not to open fresh high-beta exposure. Strong watchlist long, not an active trade.
FARTCOIN has already suffered a large downside move and funding has flipped negative, which weakens the short case. It is volatile and interesting, but not clean enough either way.
CHILLGUY still has strong momentum, but the market is thin, spready, and funding is positive. Too unstable to act on.
ZEC is one of the strongest names on the sheet with real liquidity, strong momentum, and negative funding, but it is already extended and the active veto keeps it in watch-only status.
PYTH has one of the better secondary long profiles with constructive trend and slightly supportive funding, but not enough to override the current veto.
DASH has momentum and decent volume, but the board remains neutral and funding is positive. Interesting, not actionable.
VVV has strength and okay liquidity, but the board is still neutral and funding is positive. Not a top-tier setup.
KAITO is oversold and funding is now negative, but the setup is mixed rather than clearly reversal-ready. Watchlist only.
COMP is mildly constructive but still not decisive. The board is neutral and liquidity quality is only average.
TURBO still has a valid squeeze profile with negative funding and heavy crowding, but the live veto is the wrong backdrop for opening fresh high-beta momentum trades.
GOAT is the cleaner fade on the board because it remains crowded, expensive on funding, and thin enough to break hard if momentum stalls. I would not buy this setup.
PEOPLE is too thin and too mixed to justify capital. No strong edge either way.
MAV has become more interesting on the long side with negative funding and a better squeeze profile, but the market is still too thin and the active veto keeps it below entry threshold.
MERL has some follow-through, but liquidity remains thin and the board is still neutral. Not a priority name.
MON has become momentum-positive and no longer offers a clean bearish asymmetry. It does not stand out enough on either side here.
DYM is constructive but no longer has the strong negative-funding squeeze profile that made it attractive earlier. It drops to watch-only under the current regime.
